title: Annual Information Security Policy Review and Approval Workflow

compliance.assurance_rationale: PCI DSS 12.1.2 — Reviewed: SPL provides contributing evidence for this regulatory requirement. The search query directly addresses the monitoring objective specified by the clause.
compliance.controlObjective: Evidence that PCI DSS 12.1.2 is enforced — Splunk UC-22.11.90: Annual Information Security Policy Review and Approval Workflow.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search 'UC-22.11.90' running on index=grc sourcetype="grc:policy_review" — policy_id, version, next_review_date, approver, status, archived to the restricted audit_evidence index (default 7-year retention). Auto-drafted — SME review required.

spl:
index=grc sourcetype="grc:policy_review" earliest=-730d
| where match(policy_id,"(?i)PCI|INFOSEC|ACCESS|NETWORK)")
| eval due_epoch=strptime(next_review_date,"%Y-%m-%d")
| eval overdue=if(now()>due_epoch AND NOT match(status,"(?i)approved|published"),1,0)
| stats latest(version) as ver, latest(status) as st, max(overdue) as od by policy_id
| where od=1
| table policy_id, ver, st, next_review_date
| sort policy_id
description: Tracks policy document versions, review due dates, and approver signatures so the security policy is reviewed at least annually and management acknowledges changes affecting the CDE.
value: Gives the responsible team auditor-ready evidence for PCI DSS compliance by automating regulatory compliance evidence using indexes grc and sourcetypes grc:policy_review. Replaces manual evidence gathering with a continuous Splunk search that produces structured, time-stamped artefacts suitable for regulatory examination and internal audit review.
implementation: (1) Push policy metadata whenever GRC workflow updates. (2) Link to document repository deep links. (3) Alert compliance manager 60 days before `next_review_date`. (4) Archive approved PDF hash in same event. Indexes required: `index=grc`; Sourcetypes: `sourcetype=grc:policy_review`.
visualization: Table (overdue policies), timeline (next_review_date), gauge (% policies current).
knownFalsePositives: Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
grandmaExplanation: We watch for tracks policy document versions, review due dates, and approver signatures so the security policy is reviewed at least annually and management acknowledges changes affecting the cde. This helps maintain steady compliance posture and reduces manual review effort.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk Add-on for ServiceNow (1928) or `sourcetype="grc:policy_review"`.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `index=grc` `sourcetype="grc:policy_review"` — `policy_id`, `version`, `next_review_date`, `approver`, `status`.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Push policy metadata whenever GRC workflow updates. (2) Link to document repository deep links. (3) Alert compliance manager 60 days before `next_review_date`. (4) Archive approved PDF hash in same event.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=grc sourcetype="grc:policy_review" earliest=-730d
| where match(policy_id,"(?i)PCI|INFOSEC|ACCESS|NETWORK)")
| eval due_epoch=strptime(next_review_date,"%Y-%m-%d")
| eval overdue=if(now()>due_epoch AND NOT match(status,"(?i)approved|published"),1,0)
| stats latest(version) as ver, latest(status) as st, max(overdue) as od by policy_id
| where od=1
| table policy_id, ver, st, next_review_date
| sort policy_id
```

Understanding this SPL

**Annual Information Security Policy Review and Approval Workflow (PCI DSS Req 12.1.1, 12.1.2)** — Tracks policy document versions, review due dates, and approver signatures so the security policy is reviewed at least annually and management acknowledges changes affecting the CDE.

Documented **Data sources**: `index=grc` `sourcetype="grc:policy_review"` — `policy_id`, `version`, `next_review_date`, `approver`, `status`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk Add-on for ServiceNow (1928) or `sourcetype="grc:policy_review"`. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: grc; **sourcetype**: grc:policy_review. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=grc, sourcetype="grc:policy_review", time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• Filters the current rows with `where match(policy_id,"(?i)PCI|INFOSEC|ACCESS|NETWORK)")` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **due_epoch** — applying business logic to classify, threshold, or derive compliance-relevant indicators.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **overdue** — applying business logic to classify, threshold, or derive compliance-relevant indicators.
• `stats` aggregates events by **policy_id**, producing one row per unique combination for trend analysis and threshold evaluation.
• Filters the current rows with `where od=1` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.

• Orders rows with `sort` — combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.

Output fields: due_epoch, next_review_date, od, overdue, policy_id, st, ver.

Step 3 — Validate
Confirm that events are present in the index and that the search returns expected results. Compare with known good/bad scenarios if applicable. Verify field extractions and index permissions.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Table (overdue policies), timeline (next_review_date), gauge (% policies current).
